subject: Physics
question_text: A car accelerates uniformly from rest to 72 km/h in 10 seconds. Calculate its acceleration.

subject: Physics
question_text: A force of 100N acts on a body of mass 20kg for 5 seconds. Calculate the change in momentum.
explanation: Impulse = Change in momentum = F × t = 100 × 5 = 500 Ns (or kg·m/s).

subject: Physics
question_text: A ball is thrown vertically upward with a velocity of 20 m/s. Calculate the maximum height reached. (g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: v² = u² - 2gh. At max height, v = 0. 0 = 400 - 20h → h = 20 m.

subject: Physics
question_text: Two forces of 3N and 4N act at right angles. Find the magnitude of the resultant force.
explanation: R = √(3² + 4²) = √(9+16) = √25 = 5 N. This is a 3-4-5 Pythagorean triple.

subject: Physics
question_text: A machine has a velocity ratio of 5 and efficiency of 80%. Calculate its mechanical advantage.

subject: Physics
question_text: A body of mass 2kg falls from rest through a height of 20m. Calculate its kinetic energy just before hitting the ground. (g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: By conservation of energy: KE = PE = mgh = 2 × 10 × 20 = 400 J.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the resistance of a wire of length 2m, cross-sectional area 0.5 mm², and resistivity 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ωm.

subject: Physics
question_text: A transformer has 500 turns in primary and 2500 turns in secondary. If primary voltage is 220V, find secondary voltage.
explanation: Vₛ/Vₚ = Nₛ/Nₚ → Vₛ = 220 × (2500/500) = 220 × 5 = 1100 V. Step-up transformer.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the wavelength of a wave with frequency 500 Hz traveling at 340 m/s.

subject: Physics
question_text: An object is placed 30cm from a concave mirror of focal length 20cm. Find the image distance.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the heat required to raise temperature of 2kg of water from 20°C to 100°C. (c = 4200 J/kg·K)

subject: Physics
question_text: A radioactive sample has half-life of 10 days. What fraction remains after 30 days?

subject: Physics
question_text: Two capacitors of 6μF and 3μF are connected in series. Find the equivalent capacitance.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the work done when 2 moles of an ideal gas expands isothermally at 300K from 10L to 20L. (R = 8.31 J/mol·K)

subject: Physics
question_text: A stone tied to a string of length 0.5m is whirled at 2 rev/s. Calculate the centripetal acceleration.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the de Broglie wavelength of an electron moving at 2×10⁶ m/s. (mₑ = 9.1×10⁻³¹ kg, h = 6.63×10⁻³⁴ Js)

subject: Physics
question_text: A spring of force constant 200 N/m is compressed by 0.1m. Calculate the elastic potential energy stored.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the magnetic force on a wire of length 0.5m carrying 10A placed perpendicular to a magnetic field of 0.4T.

subject: Physics
question_text: A Carnot engine operates between 500K and 300K. Calculate its efficiency.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the escape velocity from a planet of mass 6×10²⁴ kg and radius 6.4×10⁶ m. (G = 6.67×10⁻¹¹ Nm²/kg²)

subject: Physics
question_text: A body of mass 5kg moving at 4m/s collides elastically with a stationary body of mass 3kg. Find velocity of 5kg mass after collision.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the energy of a photon with wavelength 500 nm. (h = 6.63×10⁻³⁴ Js, c = 3×10⁸ m/s)

subject: Physics
question_text: A projectile is fired at 60° to horizontal with initial velocity 100 m/s. Calculate its range. (g = 10 m/s²)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the resistance of three 6Ω resistors connected in parallel.

subject: Physics
question_text: A sound wave has intensity 10⁻⁶ W/m². Calculate its intensity level in dB. (I₀ = 10⁻¹² W/m²)

subject: Physics
question_text: An electron moves perpendicular to a magnetic field of 0.5T with velocity 2×10⁷ m/s. Calculate the radius of its path. (mₑ = 9.1×10⁻³¹ kg, e = 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ C)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the torque produced by a force of 20N applied perpendicular to a spanner at 0.25m from the pivot.

subject: Physics
question_text: A body floats with 40% of its volume above water. Calculate its density. (Density of water = 1000 kg/m³)
explanation: Fraction submerged = 0.6. Density of body = 0.6 × 1000 = 600 kg/m³.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the focal length of a lens of power +2.5 D.
explanation: f = 1/P = 1/2.5 = 0.4 m = 40 cm. Positive power means convex lens.

subject: Physics
question_text: A copper wire has resistance 10Ω at 20°C. Calculate its resistance at 70°C. (α = 0.004/°C)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the angular momentum of Earth around Sun. (m = 6×10²⁴ kg, r = 1.5×10¹¹ m, T = 3.15×10⁷ s)

subject: Physics
question_text: A 100W bulb operates for 10 hours. Calculate energy consumed in kWh.
explanation: Energy = Power × Time = 0.1 kW × 10 h = 1 kWh.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the pressure at depth of 20m in water. (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 10 m/s², atmospheric pressure = 10⁵ Pa)

subject: Physics
question_text: An object is placed 10cm from a convex lens of focal length 15cm. Find the image distance and nature.
explanation: 1/v = 1/f - 1/u = 1/15 - 1/10 = (2-3)/30 = -1/30 → v = -30 cm. Negative means virtual image on same side as object.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the binding energy of deuterium nucleus. (mₚ = 1.00728 u, mₙ = 1.00867 u, m_d = 2.01355 u, 1u = 931.5 MeV)

subject: Physics
question_text: Two waves y₁ = 5 sin(ωt) and y₂ = 5 sin(ωt + π/3) interfere. Find amplitude of resultant wave.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the self-inductance of a coil in which emf of 5V is induced when current changes at 100 A/s.

subject: Physics
question_text: A gas at 27°C is heated until its volume doubles at constant pressure. Find final temperature.
explanation: Charles' law: V₁/T₁ = V₂/T₂ → 1/300 = 2/T₂ → T₂ = 600 K = 327°C.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the critical angle for light traveling from glass (n=1.5) to air.

subject: Physics
question_text: A body executes SHM with amplitude 5cm and frequency 2Hz. Calculate maximum acceleration.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the electric field at a point 0.5m from a charge of 2μC. (k = 9×10⁹ Nm²/C²)

subject: Physics
question_text: A wheel rotates at 300 rpm. Calculate its angular velocity in rad/s.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the energy stored in a capacitor of 50μF charged to 200V.

subject: Physics
question_text: A transformer has efficiency of 90%. If input power is 500W, calculate output power.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the moment of inertia of a solid sphere of mass 2kg and radius 0.5m about its diameter.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the wavelength of third line in Balmer series of hydrogen. (R = 1.097×10⁷ m⁻¹)

subject: Physics
question_text: A galvanometer of resistance 100Ω gives full-scale deflection for 1mA. Calculate shunt required to convert to 1A ammeter.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the work done in moving a charge of 5μC through a potential difference of 200V.

subject: Physics
question_text: A tuning fork of frequency 256Hz produces 4 beats per second with another fork. Find frequency of second fork.
explanation: Beat frequency = |f₁ - f₂| = 4. So f₂ = 256 ± 4 = 260 Hz or 252 Hz.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the resistivity of a wire of resistance 20Ω, length 5m, and diameter 0.5mm.

subject: Physics
question_text: An ideal gas at 2 atm and 300K expands adiabatically to 1 atm. (γ = 1.4). Find final temperature.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the force between two parallel conductors 0.5m apart carrying 10A each in same direction. (μ₀ = 4π×10⁻⁷)
explanation: F/L = μ₀I₁I₂/(2πd) = (4π×10⁻⁷×10×10)/(2π×0.5) = 2×10⁻⁵/0.5 = 4×10⁻⁵ N/m. Same direction = attractive.

subject: Physics
question_text: A 50g bullet fired at 400m/s embeds in a 1.95kg block. Find common velocity.
explanation: Conservation of momentum: m₁u₁ = (m₁+m₂)v. 0.05×400 = 2×v → v = 20/2 = 10 m/s.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the potential at a point 0.2m from a charge of 4μC. (k = 9×10⁹)

subject: Physics
question_text: A body weighs 50N in air and 40N in water. Calculate its volume. (g = 10 m/s², ρ_water = 1000 kg/m³)
explanation: Upthrust = 50 - 40 = 10 N = ρgV → V = 10/(1000×10) = 10⁻³ m³ = 1 liter.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the activity of 1g of radium-226. (Half-life = 1620 years, N_A = 6.02×10²³)

subject: Physics
question_text: A pipe open at both ends has fundamental frequency 256 Hz. If speed of sound is 340 m/s, find pipe length.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the gravitational force between two 100kg masses separated by 1m. (G = 6.67×10⁻¹¹)

subject: Physics
question_text: A ray of light passes from air to glass at 45°. If refractive index of glass is 1.5, find angle of refraction.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the energy equivalent of 1 amu in MeV. (1 amu = 1.66×10⁻²⁷ kg, c = 3×10⁸ m/s, 1eV = 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ J)

subject: Physics
question_text: A ball rolls down an incline of height 5m. Calculate its velocity at bottom. (g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: For rolling without slipping: v = √(2gh/(1+k²/R²)). For solid sphere k²/R² = 2/5. v = √(2×10×5/(1+0.4)) = √(100/1.4) = √71.4 ≈ 8.45 m/s. Actually v = √(10gh/7) = √(10×10×5/7) = √71.4 = 8.45 m/s.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the impedance of a series RLC circuit with R=30Ω, X_L=60Ω, X_C=20Ω.

subject: Physics
question_text: A body of mass 0.5kg attached to spring oscillates with period 0.2π s. Find spring constant.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the resolving power of a telescope with objective diameter 2m for light of 500nm.

subject: Physics
question_text: A 10kg mass is lifted vertically at constant speed through 5m in 2s. Calculate power. (g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: Work = mgh = 10×10×5 = 500 J. Power = Work/time = 500/2 = 250 W.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the electrostatic force between an electron and proton in hydrogen atom. (r = 5.3×10⁻¹¹ m, e = 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ C)

subject: Physics
question_text: A photon has momentum 1.0×10⁻²⁷ kg·m/s. Calculate its wavelength.

subject: Physics
question_text: A coil of 100 turns and area 0.05m² rotates at 50 rev/s in magnetic field of 0.1T. Calculate maximum emf.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the temperature at which rms velocity of oxygen equals escape velocity from Earth. (M = 32 g/mol, v_esc = 11.2 km/s, R = 8.31)

subject: Physics
question_text: In Young's double slit experiment, slit separation is 0.5mm and screen distance is 1m. If fringe width is 1mm, find wavelength.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the minimum velocity required for a body to loop a vertical circle of radius 2m. (g = 10 m/s²)

subject: Physics
question_text: A diode has forward resistance 20Ω and load resistance 1000Ω. If input voltage is 2V, calculate current.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the height of a geostationary satellite. (R = 6400 km, g = 10 m/s², T = 24h)

subject: Physics
question_text: A current of 5A flows through a wire for 2 minutes. Calculate the charge transferred.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the mutual inductance when a change of 10A in 0.1s in primary induces 50V in secondary.

subject: Physics
question_text: An electron is accelerated through 100V. Calculate its de Broglie wavelength.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the coefficient of performance of a refrigerator working between -3°C and 27°C.

subject: Physics
question_text: A wire stretched by 0.1% produces strain of 10⁻³. Calculate stress if Y = 2×10¹¹ N/m².

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the magnetic moment of a circular coil of 20 turns, radius 5cm carrying 2A.

subject: Physics
question_text: A 500g copper calorimeter contains 200g water at 30°C. 100g ice at 0°C is added. Find final temperature. (c_cu=400, c_w=4200, L=336000)
explanation: Heat available from cooling to 0°C: Q₁ = (0.5×400 + 0.2×4200)×30 = (200+840)×30 = 1040×30 = 31,200 J. Heat to melt all ice = 0.1×336,000 = 33,600 J. Since 31,200 < 33,600, not all ice melts. Final temp = 0°C.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the angular width of central maximum in single slit diffraction for slit width 0.2mm and λ=600nm.

subject: Physics
question_text: A sonometer wire of length 1m vibrates in 3 loops. If tension is 100N and mass per unit length is 1g/m, find frequency.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the energy released in fission of 1g U-235. (Energy per fission = 200 MeV, N_A = 6.02×10²³)

subject: Physics
question_text: A convex lens forms an image of same size as object when object is 20cm from lens. Find focal length.
explanation: Same size means object at 2f. So 2f = 20 cm → f = 10 cm.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the phase difference between two points 0.5m apart on a wave of wavelength 2m.

subject: Physics
question_text: A battery of emf 12V and internal resistance 2Ω is connected to a 10Ω resistor. Calculate terminal voltage.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the torque on a dipole of moment 2×10⁻⁶ Cm in uniform electric field of 10⁵ N/C at 30°.

subject: Physics
question_text: A 2kg mass moving at 3m/s collides inelastically with 1kg at rest. Find loss in kinetic energy.
explanation: v_common = (2×3)/(2+1) = 2 m/s. KE_initial = ½×2×9 = 9 J. KE_final = ½×3×4 = 6 J. Loss = 3 J.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the radius of first Bohr orbit of hydrogen. (ε₀ = 8.85×10⁻¹², h = 6.63×10⁻³⁴, m = 9.1×10⁻³¹, e = 1.6×10⁻¹⁹)

subject: Physics
question_text: A wire of resistance 16Ω is stretched to double its length. Find new resistance.
explanation: Volume constant: A₁L₁ = A₂L₂ → A₂ = A₁/2. R = ρL/A. R_new = ρ(2L)/(A/2) = 4ρL/A = 4R = 64 Ω.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the power factor of a series LCR circuit with R=40Ω, X_L=80Ω, X_C=50Ω.

subject: Physics
question_text: A Carnot engine absorbs 1000J at 500K and rejects heat at 300K. Calculate work done.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the moment of inertia of a thin rod of mass M and length L about an axis through its center perpendicular to length.
explanation: Standard formula for thin rod about center: I = ML²/12. About end: ML²/3.

subject: Physics
question_text: A projectile has maximum range 100m. Find its maximum height when fired for maximum range. (g = 10 m/s²)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the resonant frequency of an LC circuit with L=2mH and C=8μF.

subject: Physics
question_text: A 50g bullet fired at 200m/s hits a 950g block suspended by 2m string. Find maximum angle of swing.
explanation: v_common = (0.05×200)/1 = 10 m/s. h = v²/2g = 100/20 = 5m? Wait, h = L(1-cos θ). KE = mgh → ½×1×100 = 1×10×h → h=5m (impossible since L=2m). Actually: ½mv² = mgh → h = v²/2g = 100/20 = 5m. Since h>L, it will complete circle.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the potential gradient of a potentiometer wire of length 5m and resistance 10Ω carrying 0.2A.
explanation: V = IR = 0.2 × 10 = 2V. Potential gradient = V/L = 2/5 = 0.4 V/m.

subject: Physics
question_text: A particle executes SHM with amplitude A. At what displacement is KE equal to PE?
explanation: KE = PE → ½mω²(A²-x²) = ½mω²x² → A²-x² = x² → 2x² = A² → x = A/√2.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the angular momentum of electron in third Bohr orbit. (h = 6.63×10⁻³⁴ Js)

subject: Physics
question_text: A telescope has objective of focal length 100cm and eyepiece of 5cm. Calculate magnifying power for relaxed eye.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the capacitance of Earth assuming it's a spherical conductor. (R = 6400 km, ε₀ = 8.85×10⁻¹²)

subject: Physics
question_text: A body cools from 60°C to 50°C in 5 minutes. How long to cool from 50°C to 40°C? (Room temp = 30°C)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the Brewster angle for light incident from air to glass (n=1.5).

subject: Physics
question_text: A satellite orbits at height equal to Earth's radius. Calculate its orbital velocity. (R=6400km, g=10m/s²)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the energy density of an electric field of 10⁶ V/m. (ε₀ = 8.85×10⁻¹²)

subject: Physics
question_text: A 10kg block on 30° incline has coefficient of friction 0.5. Find acceleration. (g=10m/s²)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the Hall voltage for a copper strip (n=8.5×10²⁸ m⁻³) of thickness 0.2mm carrying 10A in 0.5T field.

subject: Physics
question_text: A transformer has primary of 1000 turns and secondary of 100 turns. Primary voltage is 220V. Find secondary current if primary current is 0.5A (ideal).

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the mass defect of alpha particle. (m_p=1.00728u, m_n=1.00867u, m_α=4.00150u)

subject: Physics
question_text: A 5kg block compresses a spring (k=2000 N/m) by 0.1m. If released on frictionless surface, find velocity.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the work function of a metal if light of 400nm ejects electrons with max KE of 1.1eV. (hc=1240 eV·nm)

subject: Physics
question_text: Two parallel plates 2mm apart have 100V across them. Calculate the force on an electron between them.

subject: Physics
question_text: A tuning fork of 480Hz resonates with air column of length 17.5cm in closed pipe. Find end correction. (v=336m/s)

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the magnetic field at center of circular coil of 50 turns, radius 10cm carrying 2A. (μ₀=4π×10⁻⁷)

subject: Physics
question_text: A wire of length L and resistance R is cut into 5 equal parts and connected in parallel. Find equivalent resistance.
explanation: Each part has resistance R/5. Five in parallel: 1/R_eq = 5/(R/5) = 25/R → R_eq = R/25.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the time period of a simple pendulum of length 1m on Moon. (g_moon = 1.6 m/s²)

subject: Physics
question_text: A galvanometer of resistance 50Ω gives full deflection for 2mA. Calculate series resistance for 10V voltmeter.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the stopping potential for light of frequency 8×10¹⁴ Hz on metal with work function 2eV. (h=6.63×10⁻³⁴, e=1.6×10⁻¹⁹)

subject: Physics
question_text: A 100mH inductor carries current changing at 2A/s. Calculate induced emf.

subject: Physics
question_text: Calculate the energy of electron in first excited state of hydrogen. (Ground state = -13.6 eV)
explanation: E_n = -13.6/n² eV. First excited state n=2: E₂ = -13.6/4 = -3.4 eV.
